purpose:
To establish baseline performance for customer churn prediction by training and evaluating five standard machine learning classifiers.
The data is preprocessed, balanced using SMOTE, and models are compared to set a benchmark.
The primary focus metric for comparison is Recall.
key_steps: Load raw data and drop irrelevant identifiers (RowNumber, CustomerId, Surname).
Encode categorical features using LabelEncoder (Gender) and get_dummies (Geography).
Split the dataset into training and testing sets.
Apply SMOTE to the training data to handle class imbalance.
Train and evaluate Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM models.

images.Phase1_Comparison_model_recall_compariso: A bar chart visualizing and comparing the Recall scores across the five baseline models, showing Logistic Regression at the top.

key_findings: Logistic Regression achieved the highest Recall (~69.8%), making it the best baseline for capturing actual churners.
Ensemble models (LightGBM, Random Forest, XGBoost) achieved higher overall Accuracy and ROC-AUC but struggled to match the linear model's Recall.
Decision Tree was the weakest performer overall, exhibiting the lowest Recall (~51.4%) and ROC-AUC.
next_notebook: Pending (Will be updated as subsequent notebooks are provided)
notes: SMOTE was correctly applied only to the training set to prevent data leakage. The leaderboard is explicitly sorted by Recall, indicating this is the primary optimization metric for the project.
